description: Create the DashboardLayout component with a collapsible sidebar navigation, mock data services, and all new Clinic OS pages (Admin Dashboard, Patient Management, Appointments Calendar, Billing, Staff, Settings, Notifications), then wire them all into App.tsx routing.
steps: Step 1 - Create mock data service at src/services/mockData.ts containing: (a) Mock patients array (15+ entries with id, name, email, phone, dateOfBirth, bloodGroup, gender, address, lastVisit, totalVisits, status fields), (b) Mock appointments array (20+ entries with various statuses: scheduled, in-progress, completed, cancelled, no-show), (c) Mock invoices array (10+ entries with patient references, amounts, status: paid/pending/overdue), (d) Mock staff array (8+ entries with roles: doctor/nurse/receptionist/admin), (e) Mock stats object with totalPatients, appointmentsToday, revenue, activeDoctors, (f) Mock notifications array (10+ entries with type, message, timestamp, read boolean), (g) Mock activity feed array. Export typed interfaces for all entities.

Step 2 - Create the DashboardLayout component at src/components/DashboardLayout.tsx: (a) Full-height flex layout with fixed sidebar on left and scrollable main content on right, (b) Sidebar with clinic logo/name at top, navigation links with icons (Dashboard, Patients, Appointments, Billing, Staff, Settings), active state highlighting with medical-medium color, (c) Collapsible sidebar - full width shows icon+text, collapsed shows icon only, toggle button at bottom, (d) Top header bar spanning the content area with: page title (passed as prop), search input, notification bell icon with unread count badge and dropdown showing recent notifications, user avatar/name with dropdown menu (Profile, Settings, Logout using signOutUser from AuthContext), (e) Use Shadcn Sheet component for mobile sidebar overlay, (f) Responsive: on mobile (< lg) sidebar is hidden by default and shown via hamburger menu, (g) Wrap children in the main content area with proper padding, (h) Use lucide-react icons: LayoutDashboard, Users, Calendar, Receipt, UserCog, Settings, Bell, Menu, X, LogOut, ChevronLeft, ChevronRight, (i) Import motion from 'motion/react' for smooth sidebar animation transitions.

Step 3 - Create Admin Dashboard page at src/pages/admin/AdminDashboard.tsx: (a) Wrap content in DashboardLayout with title 'Dashboard', (b) Top row: 4 stat cards in a responsive grid (1 col mobile, 2 col tablet, 4 col desktop) showing Total Patients, Appointments Today, Monthly Revenue, Active Doctors - each with an icon, value, and percentage change indicator, (c) Middle row: 2-column grid with (left) Appointments Trend line chart using recharts (LineChart with XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, Line) showing last 7 days data, (right) Revenue Overview bar chart using recharts (BarChart), (d) Bottom row: 2-column grid with (left) Recent Activity feed - list of recent events with timestamp and avatar, (right) Quick Actions grid - 4 buttons for common tasks (New Appointment, Add Patient, Create Invoice, View Reports), (e) All data from mockData.ts, (f) Use motion for card entrance animations with staggered delays.

Step 4 - Create Patient Management pages: (a) src/pages/admin/PatientList.tsx - DashboardLayout wrapper, search input at top, filter dropdown (All/Active/Inactive), responsive table (or card list on mobile) showing patient name, email, phone, last visit, status badge, action buttons (View/Edit). Include pagination (prev/next buttons with page number). Data from mockData.ts. (b) src/pages/admin/PatientDetail.tsx - DashboardLayout wrapper, patient header with avatar/name/key-info, Tabs component with tabs: Overview (summary cards for vitals, last visit, upcoming appointments), Medical History (timeline list), Appointments (filtered list of patient appointments), Prescriptions (list with download button), Billing (patient-specific invoices). Use react-router-dom useParams to get patientId. Mock all tab data.

Step 5 - Create Enhanced Appointment Management at src/pages/admin/AppointmentsPage.tsx: (a) DashboardLayout wrapper with title 'Appointments', (b) Top toolbar with date picker, view toggle (Calendar/List/Queue), and New Appointment button, (c) Calendar view: Monthly calendar grid using the existing Shadcn calendar component or a custom grid, showing appointment dots on dates. Clicking a date shows appointments for that day in a side panel. (d) List view: Filterable table of all appointments with status badges (scheduled=blue, in-progress=yellow, completed=green, cancelled=red, no-show=gray), (e) Queue view: Kanban-style board with columns for Waiting, In Progress, Completed - each showing patient name, doctor, time, with drag-drop visual (just visual cards, no actual DnD needed), (f) All views use mock data from mockData.ts.

Step 6 - Create Billing pages: (a) src/pages/admin/BillingPage.tsx - DashboardLayout wrapper, top stats row (Total Revenue, Outstanding, Paid This Month, Overdue Count), invoice list table with columns (Invoice #, Patient, Amount, Date, Status, Actions), status badges (paid=green, pending=yellow, overdue=red), filter tabs (All/Paid/Pending/Overdue), Create Invoice button. (b) src/pages/admin/CreateInvoice.tsx - DashboardLayout wrapper, form with: patient selector (dropdown), service line items (add/remove rows with service name, quantity, unit price, total), tax calculation, discount field, total display, notes textarea, Submit button. Use react-hook-form with Shadcn form components. Mock submission with toast notification.

Step 7 - Create Staff Management page at src/pages/admin/StaffPage.tsx: (a) DashboardLayout wrapper with title 'Staff Management', (b) Top bar with Add Staff button and search input, (c) Staff grid (responsive cards) showing: avatar, name, role badge (doctor=blue, nurse=green, receptionist=purple, admin=red), email, phone, status (active/inactive), (d) Click card to show detail dialog with full info, (e) Add Staff dialog/modal with form: name, email, phone, role selector, specialization (if doctor), start date. Mock submission with toast.

Step 8 - Create Settings page at src/pages/admin/SettingsPage.tsx: (a) DashboardLayout wrapper with title 'Settings', (b) Tabs: General, Working Hours, Holidays, Services. (c) General tab: Clinic name, address, phone, email inputs with save button. (d) Working Hours tab: Grid with days of week, start time and end time selectors for each day, toggle to mark day as off. (e) Holidays tab: List of holidays with date and name, Add Holiday button with date picker and name input. (f) Services tab: List of clinic services with name, duration, and price. Add/edit/delete service buttons. All mock data, save shows toast.

Step 9 - Create Notifications page at src/pages/admin/NotificationsPage.tsx: (a) DashboardLayout wrapper with title 'Notifications', (b) Filter tabs: All, Unread, Read, (c) List of notification cards with: icon based on type (appointment=Calendar, billing=Receipt, system=Bell), title, message, timestamp (relative using date-fns formatDistanceToNow), read/unread indicator (dot), (d) Mark as read / Mark all as read buttons, (e) Data from mockData.ts notifications array.

Step 10 - Update src/App.tsx to add all new routes: (a) Import all new page components, (b) Add routes wrapped in AdminRoute (or PrivateRoute for now): /admin -> AdminDashboard, /admin/patients -> PatientList, /admin/patients/:patientId -> PatientDetail, /admin/appointments -> AppointmentsPage, /admin/billing -> BillingPage, /admin/billing/create -> CreateInvoice, /admin/staff -> StaffPage, /admin/settings -> SettingsPage, /admin/notifications -> NotificationsPage. (c) Keep all existing routes unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: npm run build compiles without TypeScript errors
DashboardLayout renders a sidebar with navigation links and a top header
All 9 new pages (AdminDashboard, PatientList, PatientDetail, AppointmentsPage, BillingPage, CreateInvoice, StaffPage, SettingsPage, NotificationsPage) are created and routed
Mock data service provides typed data for all pages
All pages are responsive (work on mobile and desktop)
Charts render using recharts on the admin dashboard
Navigation between pages works via sidebar links
verification: Run `cd /projects/sandbox/cse2100 && npm run build` and confirm exit code 0 with no TypeScript errors
findings: All 11 files created successfully. Build passes with no TypeScript errors (npm run build exit code 0). Only pre-existing warning about chunk size (1868 kB main chunk). Created: src/services/mockData.ts (interfaces + data), src/components/DashboardLayout.tsx (sidebar + header layout), and 9 admin pages in src/pages/admin/. All routes wired into App.tsx wrapped in PrivateRoute. Used use-toast hook from src/hooks/use-toast.ts for toast notifications in forms.
